laws.name: Transfer of Property Act, 1882
laws.description: Section 108 governs rights and liabilities of lessor and lessee including return of premises and security deposit.

laws.name: Delhi Rent Control Act, 1958
laws.description: Governs rent, eviction, and security deposit in Delhi.

typical_steps: Document all communications with landlord in writing
Send a legal notice via registered AD post within 30 days of vacating
File complaint in Rent Control Court or Civil Court
Claim interest on delayed deposit refund at 6-12% per annum

laws.name: Consumer Protection Act, 2019
laws.description: Provides for consumer rights, deficiency of service, and complaint forums.

typical_steps: File complaint with company's grievance officer (mandatory first step)
If unresolved in 30 days, file at DCDRC (claims up to ₹50 lakh)
Attach all bills, receipts, and communication screenshots
Claim refund + compensation + litigation costs

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
laws.description: New criminal code replacing IPC. Section 318 covers cheating.

typical_steps: File FIR at nearest police station (Section 154 BNSS)
If police refuses FIR, file complaint before Magistrate
Apply for anticipatory bail if arrest feared
Engage criminal lawyer immediately

laws.name: Industrial Disputes Act, 1947
laws.description: Governs wrongful termination, retrenchment, and compensation.

laws.name: Shops and Establishments Act
laws.description: Governs service conditions for shop and establishment workers.

typical_steps: Send written representation to HR / management
File complaint with Labour Commissioner
Raise industrial dispute before Labour Court
Claim retrenchment compensation + back wages

laws.name: Code of Civil Procedure, 1908
laws.description: General civil suits, jurisdiction, and mediation.

laws.name: Limitation Act, 1963
laws.description: Time limits for filing suits (3 years for money recovery).

typical_steps: Consult a qualified advocate for case-specific advice
Gather all documentary evidence
Send legal notice as first step
File suit in appropriate court within limitation period
